prompt: A witty, cartoon-like educational illustration with clean lines and a warm color palette: a friendly cartoon machine gently squeezes a wavy ribbon of an ocean wave through a narrow funnel, and out the other side a brand new identical wave ribbon unspools and floats away as a soft glowing copy. The machine smiles with quiet pride. Style: friendly and approachable like a clever XKCD or Kurzgesagt panel, expressive faces, one clear visual gag, minimal clutter, generous negative space. Suitable for a technical textbook. No text, letters, numbers, or labels anywhere in the image.
alt: A cartoon machine funnels a wavy time series ribbon into a compact form and emits a fresh glowing copy of the same wave, showing how generative models learn to manufacture new sequences that resemble real data.
caption: Generative temporal models learn the shape of real sequences well enough to manufacture believable new ones on demand.

after_heading: 1. The Autoencoder: Encode to a Bottleneck, Decode to Reconstruct
prompt: A witty, cartoon-like educational illustration with clean lines and a warm color palette: a long colorful caterpillar of many wavy beads is gently pulled through the narrow waist of an hourglass, becoming just three glowing beads in the middle, then expands back into a nearly identical caterpillar on the far side. A small cartoon character watches the squeeze with delight. Style: clever XKCD or Kurzgesagt panel, expressive, one clear visual gag, generous negative space. Suitable for a technical textbook. No text, letters, numbers, or labels anywhere in the image.
alt: A long beaded sequence is squeezed through the narrow waist of an hourglass into a tiny handful of beads, then expands back into nearly the same sequence, picturing how an autoencoder compresses to a bottleneck and reconstructs.
caption: The bottleneck forces the model to keep only what matters, so the decoder can rebuild the whole series from a tiny summary.

after_heading: 1. The VAE: A Probabilistic Autoencoder With a Prior
prompt: A witty, cartoon-like educational illustration with clean lines and a warm color palette: instead of single sharp dots, each encoded sequence becomes a soft fuzzy cloud of probability floating in a tidy round bowl, all the fuzzy clouds nestling neatly around a calm central point like fish gathering near the middle of a pond. A cartoon character sprinkles a pinch of sparkle into one cloud and a new little wave drifts out. Style: clever Kurzgesagt panel, warm, one clear visual gag, generous negative space. Suitable for a technical textbook. No text, letters, numbers, or labels anywhere in the image.
alt: Sequences are encoded as soft fuzzy probability clouds that gather politely around a central point in a round bowl, and sampling a cloud emits a new little wave, illustrating how a VAE replaces points with distributions shaped by a prior.
caption: A VAE stores each sequence as a fuzzy cloud rather than a single point, so you can sample the latent space and get new yet plausible series.

after_heading: 3. Posterior Collapse, Beta-VAE, and Static-Dynamic Latents
prompt: A witty, cartoon-like educational illustration with clean lines and a warm color palette: a bored cartoon decoder character ignores a glowing latent cloud being offered to it and instead recites the same flat average wave from memory with its eyes closed, while the neglected latent cloud deflates sadly into a tiny puff. The output wave is suspiciously plain and identical. Style: clever XKCD panel, dry knowing humor, expressive faces, one clear visual gag, generous negative space. Suitable for a technical textbook. No text, letters, numbers, or labels anywhere in the image.
alt: A decoder character ignores the offered latent cloud and lazily recites a flat average wave from memory while the unused latent puff deflates, dramatizing posterior collapse where the model stops using its latent code.
caption: Posterior collapse is when the decoder gets lazy and ignores the latent code entirely, churning out the same bland average no matter what you sample.

after_heading: 1. The GAN Setup: Minimax, and Why Naive GANs Struggle With Dynamics
prompt: A witty, cartoon-like educational illustration with clean lines and a warm color palette: a cheeky cartoon forger paints a wavy time series ribbon trying to pass it off as real, while a squinting cartoon art inspector with a magnifying glass studies it and points out a flaw. They stand on a seesaw that tilts back and forth, neither winning, locked in a friendly endless contest. Style: clever Kurzgesagt panel, expressive faces, one clear visual gag, generous negative space. Suitable for a technical textbook. No text, letters, numbers, or labels anywhere in the image.
alt: A cartoon forger paints a fake wavy sequence while a squinting inspector with a magnifying glass spots the flaw, the two balanced on a tilting seesaw, capturing the generator versus discriminator minimax contest of a GAN.
caption: A GAN is a forger and an inspector locked in a contest: each pushes the other to improve until the fakes become hard to tell from the real series.

after_heading: 1. The Diffusion Idea: Forward Noising, Reverse Denoising
prompt: A witty, cartoon-like educational illustration with clean lines and a warm color palette: a clean wavy sequence ribbon on the left gradually dissolves into a fuzzy cloud of random static across several steps to the right, then a calm cartoon character patiently brushes the static backwards step by step, coaxing a crisp clean wave back into existence. Show the gentle gradient from order to noise and back. Style: clever Kurzgesagt panel, warm, one clear visual gag, generous negative space. Suitable for a technical textbook. No text, letters, numbers, or labels anywhere in the image.
alt: A clean wave ribbon dissolves stepwise into random static, then a patient character brushes the static backward step by step to recover a crisp wave, illustrating forward noising and reverse denoising in a diffusion model.
caption: Diffusion learns to run the clock backward: scramble a series into pure noise, then patiently denoise step by step until a clean new sequence emerges.
